div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union (Harriman) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 2281. Pravda for December 20th cites a statement by Edward CarterEdward C. Carter was president of the American Society for Russian Relief, Inc. (formerly Russian War Relief, Inc.), New York, N.Y. to the effect that during 1944 Russian War Relief intends to send supplies valued at 21 million dollars to the Soviet Union against a value of 16 million dollars this year.A handwritten notation on this telegram by Mr. Eldred D. Kuppinger, Assistant Chief of the Special Division, states that these figures were checked with the President’s War Relief Control Board, which indicated that they were accurate and that they included contributions in kind as well as in cash. It states that in the first 10 months of 1943 various medical supplies, clothing and foodstuffs to a value of 10¼ million dollars have been shipped to the Soviet Union.
